breakdown: This PR migrates complex inline GitHub Actions polling logic (~150 lines of bash) to a reusable workflow call. The architecture improves by extracting the skeptic gate logic into a reusable workflow with proper parameterization. However, the job name contains a typo ('skptic_gate'), the change removes extensive inline comments that explained the polling logic, and no tests are visible. The use of a specific commit SHA rather than a tag is good for reproducibility.
key_changes: Replaces ~150 lines of inline polling bash with single reusable workflow call
Uses specific commit SHA (@edf76e9) for reproducible workflow version
Passes pr_number, head_sha, runner_labels, concurrency_group as workflow inputs
Delegates error handling and retries to external reusable workflow
Removes inline comments documenting the polling mechanism
